title: Cursor Rules for React 19
hook: Your Cursor is still writing React 17 code. Stop that.
description: Opinionated Cursor rules for React 19 — Server Components, Server Actions, use() in Suspense boundaries, use cache directive, transitions, optimistic updates, action hooks. No generic "use TypeScript" nonsense.
previewContent:
```mdc
---
description: React 19 component rules
globs: '**/*.tsx'
alwaysApply: false
---

- Default to Server Components. Add 'use client' only when you need state, effects, browser APIs, or event handlers.
- Don't pass Server Components down as children of Client Components via props unless serialized — serialize with a well-typed interface.
- Use `use()` to unwrap promises inside a Suspense boundary instead of useEffect + setState.
```

Real React 19 patterns, not a regurgitation of the old docs.
body:
# Cursor Rules for React 19 React 19 ships new primitives (Server Components, Server Actions, `use()`, `use cache`, `useOptimistic`, `useActionState`, Form Actions, transitions, Document Metadata) and the model most LLMs were trained on is React 17-18. This ruleset corrects that. ## Install Copy each `.mdc` file into `.cursor/rules/` in your Cursor project. ## Rule 1: component-kind.mdc ```mdc --- description: Decide between Server Component and Client Component globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Component kind - Default to Server Components in the App Router. No 'use client' unless you have a reason. - 'use client' is needed when the component uses: state (useState), effects (useEffect), refs (useRef), browser APIs (window, document, localStorage), event handlers (onClick without Server Action), or React Context. - 'use client' is NOT needed just because you use the word 'component'. Server Components are components. - A Client Component can import other Client Components freely. - A Client Component CAN render Server Components — but only via props ('children') passed from a parent Server Component. Direct imports of Server Components into Client Components are not allowed. - Server Components can fetch data directly: `const data = await fetch(...)`. No useEffect, no SWR, no TanStack Query on the server. - Never pass non-serializable props (functions, class instances, Dates with timezones, Maps) from Server to Client Components. Serialize first or lift the state. ``` ## Rule 2: data-fetching.mdc ```mdc --- description: Data fetching patterns for React 19 Server Components globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Data fetching - Prefer direct fetch in Server Components. `const user = await getUser()` inside a Server Component is the right default. - For client-side data that can be read on the server, use `use()` inside a Suspense boundary: ```tsx function UserProfile({ userPromise }: { userPromise: Promise }) { const user = use(userPromise); return
{user.name}
; } ``` - Wrap data-fetching children in to get streaming. - Avoid data waterfalls: use `Promise.all` when fetching multiple things in the same component. - Never fetch on the client for data that could have been fetched on the server. ``` ## Rule 3: server-actions.mdc ```mdc --- description: Server Actions for mutations globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Server Actions - Mutations use Server Actions, not Route Handlers + fetch. - Mark with `'use server'` at the top of the file OR at the top of an individual async function inside a component. - Action functions receive FormData when invoked via `
`. - Return values are available via useActionState: ```tsx const [state, dispatch, isPending] = useActionState(myAction, initialState); return ...
; ``` - For optimistic updates, wrap state with useOptimistic: ```tsx const [optimistic, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(state, (curr, next) => [...curr, next]); ``` - Always validate input inside the Server Action — don't trust the client. - Return structured errors, not thrown exceptions, for user-visible issues. ``` ## Rule 4: cache-components.mdc ```mdc --- description: Next.js 16 use cache and cacheTag globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Cache Components (Next.js 16) - Next.js 16 introduces the 'use cache' directive for fine-grained caching. - Mark a function with 'use cache' to cache its result by arguments: ```tsx async function getUser(id: string) { 'use cache'; return db.user.findUnique({ where: { id } }); } ``` - Set invalidation windows with cacheLife('seconds' | 'minutes' | 'hours' | 'days' | 'weeks' | 'max' | custom) - Tag cached entries with cacheTag and invalidate with updateTag or revalidateTag - Do not confuse 'use cache' (Next.js 16) with unstable_cache (older pattern — migrate away) - Cache Components are compatible with Partial Prerendering (PPR) ``` ## Rule 5: transitions.mdc ```mdc --- description: useTransition and useDeferredValue for responsive UIs globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Transitions - useTransition wraps updates that can be interrupted — filter changes, tab switches, sorting. - Inside startTransition, updates are marked as non-urgent and can be interrupted by newer updates. - useDeferredValue is for deferring a value (e.g., search input) so a heavy render doesn't block typing. - Server Action dispatches are automatically inside a transition — you don't need to wrap them. ``` ## Rule 6: document-metadata.mdc ```mdc --- description: Per-page metadata in React 19 / Next.js 16 globs: '**/*.tsx' alwaysApply: false --- # Document metadata - In Next.js App Router, export a `metadata` object OR a `generateMetadata` function from any server component page.tsx or layout.tsx. - `generateMetadata` is async and receives the same params/searchParams as the page. It must run on the server. - In React 19 outside Next.js, you can render , <meta>, and <link> directly inside component bodies — React 19 hoists them to the document head automatically. - Never render <title> inside a Client Component in Next.js — use generateMetadata in the server layout instead. ``` ## Debugging hints the rules include - If you see "Attempted to serialize a function passed through a Server Component", you passed a function as a prop from server to client — remove it or lift state up. - If you see "Only plain objects, and a few built-ins, can be passed to Client Components from Server Components", same root cause. - If a Server Action doesn't run, check the 'use server' directive placement. - If useOptimistic "doesn't update immediately", make sure you're calling addOptimistic BEFORE the async Server Action, not inside a .then. ## License MIT --- *Verified against React 19.x and Next.js 16.x as of 2026-04. Update when React 19.5+ introduces new primitives.*
